name: Henry Brant
abstract: Henry Dreyfuss Brant (September 15, 1913 – April 26, 2008) was a Canadian-born American composer. An expert orchestrator with a flair for experimentation, many of Brant's works featured spatialization techniques.

works.t: Barstow: Eight Inscriptions on a California Highway Railing
U.S. Highball: A Musical Account of a Transcontinental Hobo Trip
Y.D. Fantasy -- On the Words of an Early American Tune
San Francisco: A Setting of the Cries of Two Newsboys

about_quotes.speaker: Tom McIntosh
about_quotes.evidence: He hired a man who had taught me at Juilliard. He was one of the best teachers ever, Henry Brant. He was a superb teacher, the only music person who taught composition, truly, for me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom McIntosh — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: William T. (Bill) Challis
about_quotes.evidence: Following my graduation in 1952, I began studying composition privately with Henry Brant.

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: There was Persichetti, Henry Brant, William Schumann, Peter Mennin, who was my teacher. Just an incredible staff.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: I studied composition with a person a little later on, Henry Brant.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: I studied with the very eminent American composer Henry Brant.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001
